name: Fudo Myoo
description: Fudo Myoo, also known as Acala, is a wrathful deity prominent in Shingon Buddhism and other forms of Japanese Buddhism. He is one of the Wisdom Kings (Myoo), who are emanations of the Five Wisdom Buddhas. Fudo Myoo is depicted as a fierce figure with a dark complexion, often blue or black, symbolizing the transformation of anger into salvation. He holds a sword in his right hand, which represents cutting through ignorance and illusions, and a rope in his left, used to bind those who stray from the path. He is often surrounded by flames, representing the burning away of worldly desires and impurities. Fudo Myoo's unwavering resolve and commitment to protecting sentient beings make him a powerful figure of devotion. He is believed to offer protection from negative influences, obstacles, and the cycle of rebirth. Devotees pray to Fudo Myoo for strength, courage, and the ability to overcome inner demons and external challenges. He is a symbol of unwavering faith and the transformative power of wrath.
subtitle: The Immovable Wisdom King

keyMyths.title: Acala's Vow
keyMyths.description: Fudo Myoo vowed to remain in this world until all beings are enlightened. He uses his fierce appearance to frighten those who are stubborn and resistant to the Dharma.

keyMyths.title: Cutting Ignorance
keyMyths.description: The sword held by Fudo Myoo symbolizes the cutting away of ignorance and delusion. It represents the power to sever the bonds that keep beings trapped in suffering.

keyMyths.title: Binding Evil
keyMyths.description: The rope held by Fudo Myoo is used to bind evil and prevent it from harming sentient beings. It symbolizes the control and restraint of negative forces.

sources.author: Alicia Matsunaga
